data.description:
## Why
The additive-attestations epic (dark-acme-lumen) keeps a legacy `state["signature"]` mirror
in SNAPSHOTs permanently for rollback/old-clone safety (the "expand" half of expand-contract).
Dropping that mirror is a destructive, irreversible step and must not ship until every clone
and reconcile host is confirmed upgraded.
## What
The "contract" phase: stop synthesizing the legacy `signature` mirror in new SNAPSHOTs and
remove the read-side fold-in, once a readiness gate confirms no old-version clients remain.
## Scope
`src/rebar/_commands/compact.py`, `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`, docs.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A readiness gate (e.g. fsck/version check confirming no clone predates the attestations release) is satisfied before the mirror is dropped.
- [ ] The mirror-drop release has an explicit rollback (re-enable the mirror) and a named target version.
- [ ] After contract, new SNAPSHOTs contain only `attestations`; docs note the legacy field is retired.
data.title: Contract phase: drop the legacy signature SNAPSHOT mirror (post-upgrade)
